query: I am writing a skill that wraps two shell commands — `npm test` and `npm run lint` — into a single command that runs both sequentially. Should I run eval-driven development on it before shipping?
expected_behavior: Identifies the proposed skill as a trivial one-line shell wrapper with no judgment surface (the skill either runs the two commands or it doesn't)
Declines to engage the full eval-driven workflow and explains that behavioral evals are overhead-justified only for skills that encode judgment, multi-step reasoning, or domain expertise
Recommends a lightweight alternative such as a smoke test, exit-code check, or simple manual run instead of writing 3 eval scenarios with 3 rubric items each
